title: Timepoint 2 — Readmission with recurrent PE (~1 month later)
scenarioBrief: Mr. Bell was discharged on apixaban, and an outpatient liver biopsy was arranged for about a month later. Per interventional radiology's instructions, his apixaban was held for three days before the procedure. When he arrived for the biopsy he had recurrent dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain and was sent to the emergency department. A CTA now shows pulmonary embolism in BOTH pulmonary arteries (progressed from the prior segmental clot), with a newly elevated troponin and new right-ventricular strain on echocardiography. He is tachycardic to 115 and satting 93% on 4 L nasal cannula but remains normotensive. He has been readmitted and started on a heparin infusion. Review the chart (including the interventional-radiology pre-procedure note and the new imaging) before answering.

chartGate._notes: TP2 visibility: the full chart, including the discharge summary, the outpatient hematology note, the IR pre-procedure note (which specifies the 3-day apixaban hold), and the readmission workup — CTA (bilateral, progressed PE), repeat TTE (new RV strain), repeat duplex (persistent DVT), the readmission labs (new troponin elevation), and the heparin infusion.

prompts.question: Would you recommend placement of an IVC filter in this case? State your position — you will give the arguments for and against on the next two questions.
prompts.sourceQuestion: Aliquot 4 — 'Would you recommend an IVC filter in this case?'

scoringRubric.rubricText: Decision only. Award 5 points if the resident recommends YES, place an IVC filter. (The on-case answer is Yes.) No partial credit for other stances on this sub-question. APPLICABLE MAXIMUM: 5.

prompts.question: Regardless of your answer above: what are the arguments FOR placing an IVC filter in THIS patient? Assume, for the sake of the question, that you would place one.
prompts.sourceQuestion: Aliquot 4 — 'Why would you recommend an IVC filter (assume you do)?'

scoringRubric.rubricText:
Arguments FOR an IVC filter in this specific patient. The resident is explicitly asked to argue this side; score it REGARDLESS of the recommendation they gave on the decision question. Award 1 point for each, up to 3 (any 3 = 3/3):
a) This person needs a biopsy
b) This person needs a pause of anticoagulation and can't safely pause anticoagulation
c) There are no prospective data answering the exact question, but the balance of benefits and harms suggests benefit in a scenario like this
d) The patient had a large, hemodynamically significant PE recently
e) Recurrent clot would be dangerous
APPLICABLE MAXIMUM: 3.

prompts.question: Regardless of your answer above: what are the arguments AGAINST placing an IVC filter in THIS patient? Assume, for the sake of the question, that you would not place one.
prompts.sourceQuestion: Aliquot 4 — 'Why would you NOT recommend an IVC filter (assume you do not)?'

scoringRubric.rubricText:
Arguments AGAINST an IVC filter in this specific patient. The resident is explicitly asked to argue this side; score it REGARDLESS of the recommendation they gave on the decision question. Award 1 point for each, up to 3 (any 3 = 3/3):
a) There is no hard indication in this case based on the existing data
b) Data are limited and have only compared anticoagulation alone versus anticoagulation plus filter
c) Data are absent for guidance around IVC use and anticoagulation pauses
d) An anticoagulation pause is short (and therefore feasible in this case)
e) The patient is extremely thrombogenic already, and the IVC filter increases thrombogenic risk
f) There are risks of IVC filter placement
g) There are risks of IVC filter retrieval
APPLICABLE MAXIMUM: 3.

prompts.question: More generally, what are the arguments for and against placing an IVC filter?

scoringRubric.rubricText:
Score arguments FOR and AGAINST IVC filters in general.

Arguments FOR (any 3 = 3/3):
a) Can prevent some VTE morbidity when anticoagulation is impossible/contraindicated
b) Can prevent some VTE morbidity when anticoagulation must be paused
c) Prevent VTE morbidity when anticoagulation is contraindicated in poor cardiopulmonary reserve
d) Prevent VTE morbidity when anticoagulation must be paused in poor cardiopulmonary reserve
e) Probably a reduction in PE
f) Low procedural risk in the short term for placement
g) Filters are now more consistently retrieved than years ago

Arguments AGAINST (any 5 = 5/5):
a) IVC filters are prothrombotic
b) A patient can still get PEs from clots forming on the filter
c) Filters are often left in for too long
d) Left in too long and then difficult to retrieve
e) Lack of data relevant to real clinical situations (e.g., while unable to anticoagulate)
f) Filters do not reduce VTE overall
g) Outside poor cardiopulmonary reserve, no mortality benefit
h) Low rate of retrieval
i) Poor systems to ensure retrieval
j) Adverse effects/procedural risks from placement

APPLICABLE MAXIMUM: 8 (3 for FOR + 5 for AGAINST).
